entities.name: IGBT Module
entities.description: Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistor module used in power electronics for switching and amplification.

entities.name: Failure Mode
entities.description: Different ways in which IGBT modules can fail during operation.

entities.name: Lifetime Testing
entities.description: Testing procedures to evaluate the durability and operational lifespan of IGBT modules.

entities.name: Power Electronic Converters
entities.description: Systems used for energy conversion in applications such as wind farms and photovoltaic installations, considered weak links affecting overall electrical system reliability.

entities.name: Thermo Mechanical Stress
entities.description: Combined thermal and mechanical stresses that contribute to degradation and failure of IGBT modules over time.

entities.name: Power Cycling
entities.description: A type of stress involving repeated switching on and off of power devices, contributing to wear-out and failure.

entities.name: Semiconductor Switches And Capacitors
entities.description: Most vulnerable components in power converters, prone to failure due to overloads, transients, thermal stress, mechanical vibrations, and humidity.

entities.name: Motor Drives
entities.description: Systems that may operate in harsh environments unknown at manufacturing, leading to mismatch between qualification tests and field conditions, causing power switches to fail before target lifetime.

entities.name: Power Switches
entities.description: Components in motor drives responsible for switching electrical power.

entities.name: Motor Drive
entities.description: System that controls the operation of electric motors using power electronic components.

entities.name: Power Converter
entities.description: Device that converts electrical power from one form to another, incorporating IGBT modules.

entities.name: IGBT Lifecycle
entities.description: Stages through which an IGBT module passes from qualification to operation and end-of-life.

entities.name: Qualification
entities.description: Initial testing and validation of IGBT modules at the manufacturer site.

entities.name: Storage And Transportation
entities.description: Phase where IGBT modules are stored and transported to the power converter manufacturer.

entities.name: Converter Assembly And Testing
entities.description: Stage where IGBT modules are assembled into converters and tested for qualification.

entities.name: Commissioning And Operation
entities.description: Final stage where the power converter is installed at the customer site and operated.

entities.name: Mechanical Shock
entities.description: Physical impact that can affect IGBT module reliability before commissioning.

entities.name: Temperature Shock
entities.description: Rapid temperature changes that can affect IGBT module reliability before commissioning.

entities.name: Incorrect Handling And Assembly
entities.description: Improper treatment of IGBT modules that may cause early failures.

entities.name: Random System Overstress Events
entities.description: Unexpected electrical stresses such as overvoltage and overcurrent that can cause IGBT failure.

entities.name: Wear Out Failure Modes
entities.description: Long-term degradation mechanisms leading to IGBT module failure under sustained loading.

entities.name: End Of Life
entities.description: Point at which IGBT modules fail and no longer meet target lifetime.

entities.name: Converter Design, Control, And Protection
entities.description: Improvements made by power converter manufacturers to reduce effects of overstress and extend module lifetime.

relationships.description: IGBT modules exhibit various failure modes during their operational life.
Lifetime testing is performed to assess the reliability and expected operational life of IGBT modules.
IGBT modules are key components within power electronic converters.
Failures in power electronic converters significantly influence the reliability of the entire electrical system.
Various failure modes including overloads, transients, thermal stress, mechanical vibrations, and humidity cause IGBT module failures.
Lifetime testing methods assess the long-term reliability and degradation of IGBT modules.
Thermo-mechanical stress is a primary factor causing degradation in IGBT modules.
Power cycling induces wear-out failures in power electronic components.
Semiconductor switches and capacitors are the most vulnerable components within power electronic converters.
Motor drives often operate in harsh environments that are difficult to predict at manufacturing.
Qualification tests may not accurately represent the harsh conditions experienced in the field.

relationships.source: Mismatch Between Test And Field Conditions
relationships.sourceCanonicalName: mismatch between test and field conditions
relationships.target: Power Switch Failures Before Target Lifetime
relationships.targetCanonicalName: power switch failures before target lifetime
relationships.description: Differences between test conditions and actual field environments cause power switches to fail prematurely.
IGBT lifecycle starts with qualification at the manufacturer site.
IGBT modules go through storage and transportation before reaching the converter manufacturer.
IGBT modules are assembled into converters and tested for qualification.
Final stage where the converter is commissioned and operated at the customer site.
Mechanical shock events before commissioning can reduce module reliability.
Temperature shock events before commissioning can reduce module reliability.
Improper handling and assembly can cause early failures.
Overvoltage and overcurrent events can cause random failures.
Long-term degradation under sustained loading leads to failure.
These failure mechanisms can cause IGBT modules to fail before target lifetime.
Improvements aim to reduce the impact of overstress and demanding mission profiles.
